title: UTF-8 cursor boundary validation on text editing
description:
GOOD CHOICE: Backspace, Left, and Right arrow handlers validate UTF-8 char boundaries with loop-based search (event.rs 744-751, 759-775). Before decrementing cursor position, the code checks is_char_boundary() and walks backwards if needed.

A naive implementation would just do cursor -= 1, which silently breaks multi-byte UTF-8 characters (e.g. emoji, accented chars). The boundary validation is non-obvious and easy to overlook.

This is load-bearing for correct text editing with Unicode input.
close_reason: Verified invariant holds; nie-5xf0 documented in DESIGN.md §16 UTF-8 cursor boundary invariant; nie-pjxx and nie-h0ci already documented in DESIGN.md
